notes:
Sướng means feeling really good, pleasurable, or super comfortable. It’s stronger and more physical than just “happy.”

It’s an adjective, used for both body and emotions — good food, good rest, easy life, or emotional satisfaction can all make you sướng. Often used with quá, lắm, ghê to intensify.

It can also imply “living comfortably” or “having it easy.” Compare: vui = happy/cheerful, hạnh phúc = deep happiness, sướng = immediate pleasure or comfort. Opposite: khổ, cực (suffering, struggling).
exampleSentences: Khi nhận được quà sinh nhật, em cảm thấy rất sướng.
Sau kỳ nghỉ dài ở biển, anh thấy cuộc sống thật sướng.
Chị ấy luôn mỉm cười vì lúc nào cũng sướng với công việc hiện tại.

example.vi: Hôm nay tôi cảm thấy sướng vì được nghỉ ngơi.
example.en: Today I feel happy because I get to rest.
example.es: Hoy me siento feliz porque puedo descansar.

definitions.es: Una sensación de felicidad o placer.
definitions.en: A feeling of happiness or pleasure.
definitions.vi: Cảm giác hạnh phúc hoặc vui vẻ.

exampleSentencesTranslated: When I received a birthday present, I felt very happy.
After a long holiday at the beach, I feel that life is really happy.
She always smiles because she is always happy with her current job.
notes_md:
**Sướng** means feeling really good, pleasurable, or super comfortable. It’s stronger and more physical than just “happy.”

It’s an adjective, used for both body and emotions — good food, good rest, easy life, or emotional satisfaction can all make you *sướng*. Often used with *quá*, *lắm*, *ghê* to intensify.


It can also imply “living comfortably” or “having it easy.” Compare: *vui* = happy/cheerful, *hạnh phúc* = deep happiness, *sướng* = immediate pleasure or comfort. Opposite: *khổ*, *cực* (suffering, struggling).

translation.en: I feel happy when winning the match.

content.sentence: Tôi cảm thấy ___ khi thắng trận.

translation.en: I feel happy when being helped.

content.sentence: Tôi cảm thấy ___ khi được giúp đỡ.
